description: Ironworker and erection safety — fall protection, steel erection OSHA requirements, crane safety, rigging safety, structural collapse prevention, and the hazards specific to working at height with heavy steel.

fall_protection.OSHA_requirement: 100% fall protection required at 6 feet above lower level in steel erection (OSHA 1926 Subpart R). No exceptions.

personal_fall_arrest.components: Full body harness + lanyard or SRL + anchor point rated for 5000 lbs per person.
personal_fall_arrest.free_fall: Maximum 6 feet free fall distance. Deceleration distance adds another 3.5 feet. Need 18.5 feet of fall clearance below anchor.
personal_fall_arrest.inspection: Inspect harness and lanyard before each use. Reject if: frayed webbing, damaged stitching, deformed D-ring, cracked buckle, cut lanyard.

SRL.description: Self-Retracting Lifeline. Automatically retracts slack, limits free fall to inches.
SRL.advantage: Less free fall than standard lanyard. Better for work at lower heights where clearance is limited.
SRL.classes: Class A (personal fall arrest), Class B (personal fall arrest + leading edge)

guardrail.requirements: 42 inch top rail, 21 inch mid-rail, toe board. Can be cable rail with 200 lb test.

safety_nets.placement: Within 30 feet of walking/working surface. Used when other systems are impractical.

connectors.description: Ironworkers engaged in connecting steel (bolting beams to columns) at heights up to 30 feet.
connectors.special_rule: OSHA allows connectors to be protected by personal fall arrest OR positioning device system while making initial connection. Must have fall protection available at all times.

leading_edge.description: Work at an unprotected edge where steel is being erected.
leading_edge.requirement: Fall arrest system with Class B SRL, or safety net, or controlled access zone (for specified activities only).

controlled_access_zone.use: Leading edge work only. Area between leading edge and 6 feet back. Controlled by competent person. Workers must use personal fall arrest.

fall_protection.practical_note: On steel erection, 100% tie-off means ALWAYS connected. Moving between anchor points requires double lanyard (maintain 100% connection during transfer). No exceptions, no matter how experienced you are.

steel_erection_OSHA.subpart_R: OSHA 1926.750-761. Specific rules for steel erection.
steel_erection_OSHA.site_layout: Erection area must be controlled — barricade the area below steel erection. No non-essential personnel under active erection.
steel_erection_OSHA.column_stability: Columns must have minimum 4 anchor bolts. Column cannot be released from crane until 4 bolts are installed and tightened snug-tight minimum.
steel_erection_OSHA.beam_connection: Beams must have minimum 2 bolts installed at each connection before crane releases load. Bolts must be wrench-tight minimum.
steel_erection_OSHA.double_connections: Where a beam is field-bolted on both ends and one end is on column web: the beam seat (clip angle, plate) on the double-connection side must be shop-installed. Worker must not be in position where loss of support on one end can cause fall.
steel_erection_OSHA.plumbing_up: Columns must be plumbed and braced before loading with beams. Temporary bracing per erection plan. Do not remove bracing until permanent bracing or decking is in place.

decking.requirement: Metal decking must be installed as close behind erection as possible. Max 8 floors between erection and decking.
decking.bundle_placement: Deck bundles placed on structural members rated for the load. Do not overload any single member.
decking.edge_protection: Perimeter safety cable at column lines where decking is not yet installed.

steel_erection_OSHA.hoisting_and_rigging: All hoisting must follow crane manufacturer load chart. Rigging must be inspected by competent person. Tag lines on all loads.

signal_person.requirement: Qualified signal person required when operator cannot see the load or landing area. Signal person must be in clear view of operator OR use radio communication.
signal_person.qualifications: Qualified by third-party evaluator or employer's program. Must know ASME B30.5 standard hand signals.

critical_lifts.definition: Lift over 75% of crane capacity, over occupied areas, near power lines, blind picks, tandem lifts, lifts with more than one crane.
critical_lifts.requirements: Written lift plan. Engineer review. Pre-lift meeting with all involved personnel. Designated signal person and rigger.

load_chart.rule: Operator must verify every lift against the load chart BEFORE lifting. Load + rigging weight + block weight must not exceed chart capacity at the working radius.
load_chart.factors: Radius, boom length, boom angle, on outriggers or rubber, ground conditions, wind speed.

crane_safety.outriggers: Fully extend outriggers for all lifts unless manufacturer allows partial extension. Pads under outrigger floats — minimum 3x the float area. Check ground bearing capacity.
crane_safety.swing_radius: Barricade the swing radius of the crane tail/counterweight. Workers crushed between counterweight and fixed objects is a leading cause of crane fatalities.

power_lines.minimum_clearance: 20 feet for lines up to 350 kV. 50 feet for lines above 350 kV. Or de-energize and ground lines.
power_lines.rule: If you can see power lines from the crane, you need a spotter and a plan.

crane_safety.wind: Most cranes must cease operations at 20-30 mph sustained wind. Check manufacturer limit. Lattice boom cranes have lower wind limits for boom erection/disassembly.

wire_rope.inspection: 6 randomly broken wires in one rope lay, or 3 in one strand. Kinking, bird-caging, core protrusion, corrosion. Reject if found.
wire_rope.capacity: Depends on diameter, hitch type, and angle. Vertical hitch is 100%. Choker is 75%. Basket at 60 degrees is 86%.

synthetic_web.inspection: Cuts, tears, snags, burns, chemical damage, exposed core yarns, damaged fittings. Reject if found.
synthetic_web.NOT_for: Temperatures above 180F or around sharp edges without protection.

alloy_chain.grade: Grade 80 or Grade 100 only for overhead lifting. NEVER use Grade 30 or Grade 70 (binding chain) for lifting.
alloy_chain.inspection: Stretched links, bent/cracked links, gouges, wear exceeding 10% of link diameter. Reject if found.

sling_angles.30_degrees: 50% of rated capacity — MINIMUM safe angle
sling_angles.rule: NEVER rig with sling angle less than 30 degrees from horizontal. The horizontal force component becomes extreme and can break the sling or pull the load apart.

rigging_safety.shackle_rules: Load rated on body. Never exceed WLL. Pin must be fully threaded and secured (cotter pin type) or hand-tight (screw pin type). Never shock-load a shackle. Never side-load a shackle.
rigging_safety.tag_lines: Required on every suspended load to control swing and rotation. Minimum 3/8 inch manila or polypropylene rope. NEVER wrap around hand or body.

structural_collapse.temporary_bracing: Bracing plan must be designed by engineer and followed exactly. Temporary bracing prevents structural collapse during erection before permanent connections are complete.
structural_collapse.load_path: Every piece of steel must have a load path to the foundation. If the load path is not complete (missing bracing, missing connections), the structure can collapse.
structural_collapse.wind_during_erection: Partially erected steel is more vulnerable to wind than completed structure. Temporary bracing must account for wind loads during erection.
structural_collapse.competent_person: OSHA requires a competent person for steel erection who can identify hazards and has authority to take corrective action.
